programme.title: Café Con Sabor a Mi Tierra
programme.desc: La historia de una familia cafetalera cuya cosecha se ve amenazada por una terrible plaga y los bajos precios.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Mel siente presión por parte de su familia para cumplir sus expectativas. Jade intenta convencer a Khadijja de estudiar una carrera, pero Ali le pide que tenga cuidado con lo que dice. Mustafá llega para ayudar a Mohamed con la tienda.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Mohamed llega a casa con Amin y le cuenta a Latiffa lo que su hijo estaba haciendo. Jade no quiere ir a Brasil con Said. Mel llega con Ceceu, pero lo ve con otras chicas y se va llorando.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Alicinha llega a casa de Edna mientras Albieri se desespera porque se cortó la llamada de Léo. Miro avisa que Xande desafiará a Vitor. Latiffa se acuerda de Jade con la actitud tan rebelde de Samira.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Telminha alienta a Mel para que siga intentando algo con Ceceu, pero Mel dice que ahora con los exámenes no tiene tiempo de pensar en novios. Said y su familia se despiden de Ali y dejan a Nazira en su casa.

programme.title: La tierra prometida
programme.desc: Tibar le dice a su rey que toda la gente está preocupada por el ataque de Josué. Josué le cuenta a Caleb el plan que Dios le contó para entrar a Jericó.

programme.title: La tierra prometida
programme.desc: Elieber y Aruna son atacados, Josué los salva y los lleva a un lugar seguro. Leia se pone muy mal al enterarse que Elieber está herido. Leia y Aruna culpan a Aruna por lo que le pasó a Elieber.

programme.title: La tierra prometida
programme.desc: Tibar está a punto de matar a Rajab, pero la deja escapar para pasar una noche con ella. Maquir le confiesa a Livana que está enamorado de ella, pero sabe que no pueden estar juntos.

programme.title: El regalo de Dios
programme.desc: Kim Soo Hyun vive su peor pesadilla cuando su hija es secuestrada y asesinada. En el momento en que intenta quitarse la vida, un suceso mágico sucede y regresa 14 días atrás, antes de la muerte de su hija con la oportunidad de cambiar la historia.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Adaptación de uno de los libros más reconocidos de la Biblia, que aborda la historia del origen de la humanidad, invitando a entender el presente, volviendo al principio de todo. La caída de Lucifer, la vida de Adán y Eva, Noé y el diluvio, y otros.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: María do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón, pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Cuneyt habla con Görkem y le dice que ha decidido retomar su amistad con Mahinur. Reyha observa a Serkan y le da el pésame. Serkan amenaza a Melissa. Metin teme que no les permitan adoptar a Melissa.

programme.title: Moisés y los diez mandamientos
programme.desc: La historia de Moisés desde su nacimiento y llegada a manos de la princesa Henutmire hasta llevar a su pueblo a la Tierra Prometida. Su crianza como un verdadero príncipe egipcio junto a su tío Ramsés.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Telminha alienta a Mel para que siga intentando algo con Ceceu, pero Mel dice que ahora con los exámenes no tiene tiempo de pensar en novios. Said y su familia se despiden de Ali y dejan a Nazira en su casa.

programme.title: Destinos cruzados
programme.desc: Toprak despierta de un coma tras cinco años sin recordar nada de su pasado, y regresa a una familia que no reconoce, incluida su prometida de toda la vida y un niño que vive con él. Sin embargo, su amnesia lo mantiene en una incertidumbre constante.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Latifa le cuenta a Jade que Lucas tiene varias amantes. Amin se roba unos productos de la tienda de su padre para venderlos. Edna le agradece a Alicinha por todos sus buenos gestos. Ali le pide a Zoraide que lo siga para no estar solo con Nazira.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Melissa le dice a su tía que la quiere como una madre. Mahinur y Melissa hacen un pastel para agradecer a Cuneyt. Defne le dice a Metin que puede confiar en ella y que si la policía se entera del incidente, Melissa correría peligro.

programme.title: Destinos cruzados
programme.desc: Toprak despierta de un coma tras cinco años sin recordar nada de su pasado, y regresa a una familia que no reconoce, incluida su prometida de toda la vida y un niño que vive con él. Sin embargo, su amnesia lo mantiene en una incertidumbre constante.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Latifa le cuenta a Jade que Lucas tiene varias amantes. Amin se roba unos productos de la tienda de su padre para venderlos. Edna le agradece a Alicinha por todos sus buenos gestos. Ali le pide a Zoraide que lo siga para no estar solo con Nazira.

programme.title: El regalo de Dios
programme.desc: Kim Soo Hyun vive su peor pesadilla cuando su hija es secuestrada y asesinada. En el momento en que intenta quitarse la vida, un suceso mágico sucede y regresa 14 días atrás, antes de la muerte de su hija con la oportunidad de cambiar la historia.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Adaptación de uno de los libros más reconocidos de la Biblia, que aborda la historia del origen de la humanidad, invitando a entender el presente, volviendo al principio de todo. La caída de Lucifer, la vida de Adán y Eva, Noé y el diluvio, y otros.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: School is coming to a close and vacation season is getting underway. Tonight, some ideas to keep the troops entertained! Golf in the city? Shayna Seymour gets some work in on her short game at a high-tech mini golf course, and swings big at an indoor golf course in Boston. Nicole Estaphan does a deep dive into historic video games in Rhode Island, while Erika Tarantal is aiming for the bullseye at a darts emporium. Board games, pinball, and more options to keep everyone active and entertained and now has 50 triathlons under his belt.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Are you ready to get a little bit spooky? We are getting in the mood for Halloween with a little history on how it got to where it is today. We walk you through some haunts in Salem, some well-known, and some not. Salem isn’t the only spot with ghostly ties; we get a tour of some of Boston’s haunted spots, including the Boston Common. Ted Reinstein takes a trip with paranormal investigators aboard an (allegedly) haunted ship in Mystic, Conn. And if you’re in the mood to experience your own Halloween thrills, we visit two local attractions still scaring visitors year after year.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: As temperatures drop, you may be hunting for indoor activities. Pac-Man and pinball enthusiasts, rejoice! In Norwood, a retro video game enthusiast has built his dream business, Hometown Arcade.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: Bringing Down The House: The Bombing of Harvey's Casino
programme.desc: In the summer of 1980, two men wheeled what looked like an IBM computer into a Lake Tahoe casino. The events that followed rocked the industry, baffled bomb technicians, and changed the lives of a criminal’s and a casino magnate’s family forever.

programme.title: The Lost Children of Carlisle
programme.desc: A powerful look at the history of Native American boarding schools as told through the stories of tribal members and their surviving family members, who endured an educational system founded on the concept “Kill the Indian in him, and save the man.”

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we learn about the day jobs of local comedians, from New Hampshire’s Juston McKinney (a onetime sheriff’s deputy in Maine) to a Boston-area comic who helps families plan funerals, and another who is – believe it or not – a taxidermist.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Couldn’t we all use a laugh? Tonight we connect with local comics who find “funny” everywhere they look. We meet up with Worcester teacher-turned-standup Orlando Baxter, and talk to Stephanie Peters, who used laughter to raise funds for the people of Ukraine. We check out a brand new comedy club in Beverly and connect with former Saturday Night Live writer Sam Jay, who reflects on her Dorchester roots. And is it possible to teach funny? At Emerson College, we find out.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: The traditional workplace is changing and businesses and employees are changing with it. We visit a company that has pivoted (permanently) to a “remote-centric” workplace, and check out a new apartment complex in Quincy that’s marketing itself to the “WFH” crowd. We also explore the child care challenge and hear from Massachusetts Gov. Charlie Baker about his administration’s Future of Work report.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight, Shayna Seymour catches up with AJ Dybantsa. Also, Anthony Everett challenges an 8-year-old national chess champ to a match, while Erika Tarantal takes a lesson from the youngest-ever senior world champion in fencing!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: When two Northeastern University students felt excluded from an activity they loved they created a welcoming community of their own with Lonely Bones Skate Co., while pastry chef Gloria Cabral has won awards for her own talent but she is most proud of mentoring a new generation of pastry chefs. Tonight’s edition of “Gamechangers” focuses on people who are quietly making a difference right here in our community – from a Dorchester artist creating a new “face” for playing cards to a team making the sport of tennis accessible to all.

programme.desc: Mikey’s getting married soon and the clock is ticking to get his teeth picture-perfect. Lynn’s embarrassed by the color of her teeth and won’t wait another moment for a white smile. Can the FIXT team get the work done in time?

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Anthony Everett tours a unique home for sale, where the proceeds are targeted for charity thanks to the wishes of its prominent late owner. We travel to Great Barrington, where a couple has lovingly restored a chapel on the grounds of a former private school, built in memory of three members of the community who died tragically – and now a respite for hikers along the Appalachian Trail. In Concord, Anthony dines at a special cafe staffed by incarcerated individuals who are learning new skills. And in Brighton, we visit a local co-op community that supports young adults who are experiencing homelessness.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle's award-winning team brings you the stories of New England.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle is going a little nutty tonight over NUTS! Cwcashews, almonds, peanuts, even acorns are on the menu. We visit Arcade Snacks in Auburn and Q’s Nuts in Somerville for their veritable nut buffets and choices. We learn about the benefits of acorn flour and how to make it. We are also introduced to high-powered almond butter, courtsy of local Bear Butter and discover how local researchers are trying to tame nut allergies in patients.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Explore Retro pieces from the past; from roller rinks, fabric wrapping paper and typewriters, find out what’s making a comeback.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: It’s New Hampshire’s second-largest county, home to some of its best-known towns, and to famed Franconia Notch. Tonight Ted Reinstein shares the autumn beauty of Grafton County, N.H. He discovers how “The Notch” was once saved from development, and explores scenic views along the Cross-New Hampshire Adventure Trail. Hard to believe it’s been 20 years since the Old Man in the Mountain crumbled; Ted meets the team who designed “profilers” that help simulate the rocky view. And at Polly’s Pancake Parlor, Ted digs into a half-stack and catches up with old friends.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: If you love all things 19th Century (think Bridgerton or Downton Abbey), impeccable gardens, and antique decor, then you will enjoy tonight’s trip to some of the area’s great estates. We begin at Glen Magna Farms in Danvers. Now an events venue and public park, the estate was once the summer retreat for the Peabody and Endicott families (purchased for a mere $4,000). We tour the Nichols House Museum, a Beacon Hill brownstone whose former owner inspired a musical. Also tonight, prepare to be awed by the grounds at Blithewold, an estate that was only owned by women.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: A look at art, cuisine, and advocacy, all infused with culture in celebration of Hispanic Heritage Month. We take a look at removing the stigma around accessing mental health resources with a counselor with Venezuelan roots and talk with a Seekonk woman using arts and crafts to do the same. We meet a husband and wife from Cambridge sharing cultural stories through their Pan-Latin music. Anthony Everett learns the art of empanada-creation with a local Argentinian chef and we catch up with a Brazillian artist focused on creating sustainable fashion with nature.

programme.title: LIVE: WCVB NewsCenter 5
programme.desc: The latest news, weather, and sports from the WCVB NewsCenter 5 Team.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Just six miles from downtown Boston, Malden is experiencing a renaissance. We stop by District Kitchen for handmade noodles and dumplings and check out the local art at Gallery at 57. Meanwhile, if you think you know Paul Revere’s turf, we have news for you from the town of Canton – where we hit the Paul Revere Heritage Site and Northern Spy restaurant, which focuses on locally-sourced ingredients. Finally, Nicole Estaphan heads to the Metrowest community of Holliston where she meets a father-daughter artist team and submerges herself in a minus-200-degree cryotherapy tank.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle

Up in Salem, we’re bypassing the “witch thing” to check out the city’s other attractions, including its annual film festival. Finally, a visit to Norwood - where a huge vintage market is a big draw for shoppers and a new grocer is transforming the shopping chore.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Massachusetts has dozens of “mill towns” and tonight we’re exploring how some of these industrial-era buildings are serving a new generation – offering everything from freshly-roasted coffee to fine art.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: Historic Harwich is heaven for beach lovers…but it’s also filled with history, abundant natural beauty, and artists working to keep Cape Cod traditions alive. Erika Tarantal meets a local carver who makes duck decoys in the tradition of a renowned local craftsman. We visit a cranberry bog and a lavender farm, and at a Harwich Mariners game, meet the folks trying to bring the Cape Cod Baseball League’s Hall of Fame back to life. Finally, no trip to the Cape is complete without some reading material; we hit a local stop along the Cape Cod Bookstore Trail.

programme.title: Searching for Johnny
programme.desc: (2022) The disappearance of Johnny Gosch is one of Iowa's longest running, and most notorious mysteries. In 1982, the 12-year-old paperboy in West Des Moines vanished without a trace. Forty years later, KCCI uncovers where the investigation stands.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: February is American Heart Month, and Nicole Estaphan is sharing personal stories that stress the importance of awareness, early screening, and prevention. Nicole meets a transplant recipient who had no warning of his life-threatening disease, and a young mom who suffered a heart attack while pregnant – one of a growing number of younger patients who experience a cardiac event. She talks to doctors about risk and the importance of a heart-healthy lifestyle. Finally, Nicole shares her own diagnosis of a cardiac condition that affects 20 million American adults.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Hidden Gems aren’t hard to find in New England (if you know where to look!). Tonight we follow the breadcrumbs to a French bakery tucked into a liquor store in Milford. Forget candlepins - we’re checking out a duckpin bowling alley, and dining on the “Mystery Monday” menu at Metacom Kitchen in Warren, R.I.. And tucked into a former Methodist Church in Charlton, Mass., is a sewing “sanctuary” that’s still lifting spirits. Plus, we head to Gillette Stadium to meet the New England Revolution -- a record-breaking team that’s been hiding in plain sight.

programme.desc: WCVB

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we’re back on the hunt for hidden history. Anthony Everett visits Brockton where he rides on a historic fire engine that battled the devastating Strand Theatre fire in 1941, and a local museum remembers the 13 lives lost. We take a stroll with the author of “Boston’s Oldest Buildings and Where to Find Them.” And tucked away among the storefronts inside the Prudential Center are actual relics of three Catholic saints.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we’re back on the hunt for hidden history. programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: When is a bait shop more than a bait shop? When it holds generations’ worth of local history, in the form of cherished photographs. For decades, the late owner of Surfland on Plum Island photographed locals who had landed huge fish, and her family continues the tradition today. We also meet a group of Italian divers who traveled to the U.S. to explore the Andrea Doria, the ocean liner that sank off Nantucket in 1956 and has intrigued divers ever since. And in the tiny town of West Townsend, we peruse the largest collection of antique American tall case clocks for sale in the United States.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Ted Reinstein is looking up, down, around, and above to discover quirky systems, codes, and features around Boston that are “hiding in plain sight!” Parts of the city are encircled by a high-wire “eruv” – Ted will tell you what that is. We meet a Boston man who makes art in out-of-the-way places (and manages to live rent-free in the city). Boston officials share the story behind those tiny colored plastic disks embedded in city streets. And while the 1,200 fire alarm boxes in Boston may look like a relic - they work! We meet the team charged with maintaining them.
